findings.description: yaml.load is called without SafeLoader, allowing arbitrary Python object deserialization (CWE-502) and potential code execution from a tampered config file; should be yaml.safe_load.
On PyYAML >= 6.0 yaml.load(f) without a Loader argument raises TypeError, so _read_file crashes on any modern PyYAML install.
Merge order contradicts the documented precedence: _read_file is applied after _read_env, so file values override environment variables instead of env winning.
dict(DEFAULTS) is a shallow copy, so merged['feature_flags'] aliases the module-level DEFAULTS dict and any caller mutation pollutes all subsequent loads.
get() re-runs the full load() (disk stat/read, YAML parse, whole-environ scan, INFO log) on every single-key access with no caching, multiplying I/O and log spam via require() and feature_enabled().

feature_enabled assumes feature_flags is a dict, but an env override (always a scalar after _coerce) or a YAML null/scalar value makes flags.get raise AttributeError, and the {} fallback in get() never applies because DEFAULTS always supplies the key.
Malformed YAML raises an uncaught yaml.YAMLError out of load(), inconsistent with the graceful handling of a missing file and a non-mapping root.
_coerce aggressively retypes values with no escape hatch to force strings: numeric secrets become int, 'nan'/'inf' become floats, and int('1_0') silently becomes 10.
Every APP_-prefixed env var is merged unvalidated, so typos (e.g. APP_WORKR_COUNT) silently add junk keys instead of overriding or warning, and unrelated APP_ vars leak into the config.
An explicit null in the file yields None for an existing key so get() bypasses the fallback, and require() then misreports the explicitly-set null as a missing key.
TOCTOU race between os.path.exists and open: if the file disappears in between, FileNotFoundError propagates even though a missing file is defined as non-fatal; try/except open would be robust.
An empty config file parses to None and is logged at ERROR as a non-mapping root, although an empty file should arguably be treated like a missing file (warning or silent empty dict).
Env names are lowercased, so config keys containing uppercase or mixed case can never be overridden via environment variables.
Optional[Any] is redundant since Any already includes None; the annotation adds noise without typing value.
The INFO log claims the config was 'loaded from' the path even when the file was missing or invalid, and the key count includes defaults and env keys, which is misleading.
ConfigLoader and several public methods (require, feature_enabled) lack docstrings.
